mnemonics.title: Types of Graphs
mnemonics.content: CVPB: Complete, Regular, Bipartite - remember 'Very Popular Graphs Have Clever Names' for Complete-Vertextypes, Regular-degree, Bipartite-sets

mnemonics.title: Graph Properties
mnemonics.content: Dijkstra = D-I-J-K-S-T-R-A: Directed, Integer weights, Jumps to min, Keeps track, Sorts by distance, Takes smallest, Removes when done, All vertices reached

mnemonics.title: Adjacency Matrix vs List
mnemonics.content: AML: Adjacency Matrix for Lookups (O(1)), Adjacency List for Space-saving (O(V+E))

mnemonics.title: Graph Degrees Handshaking
mnemonics.content: In out, both ways: In-degree + Out-degree = 2×E for directed (Handshaking Lemma)

acronyms.usage: In V = {v1, v2, v3}, V represents the vertex set
E = {e1, e2, e3} represents the edge set in a graph
K5 is a complete graph with 5 vertices, 10 edges

acronyms.expansion: Complete bipartite graph with m and n vertices in each part
acronyms.usage: K3,3 is a complete bipartite graph with partitions of size 3 and 3
Explores graph level by level, finds shortest path in unweighted graph
Explores graph depth-wise, uses stack/recursion, detects cycles
Tree spanning all vertices with minimum total weight

visualTips.visualization: Draw a pentagon with all diagonals filled - looks like a star inside a pentagon. Every vertex connects to every other.
Split vertices into two colors (say red and blue) with edges only going between colors, never within the same color.
Imagine cities as dots connected by roads labeled with distances. Shortest path is the route with minimum total distance label.
Think of a 2D checkerboard where rows and columns are vertices. Fill 1 where vertices are friends (adjacent).
Each vertex gets its own shopping list of neighbors. Vertex A's list shows everyone A can directly reach.

analogies.analogy: Think of a social network: people are vertices, friendships are edges connecting them.
Like a map app: cities are vertices, roads are edges, distances/travel times are weights on edges.
In a party, a person's 'degree' is how many other people they shake hands with.
A dating app matching men and women - edges only go between groups, never within the same group.
Driving from one city to another: the route is a path through intermediate cities (vertices) on connected roads (edges).
Like setting up internet in all rooms with minimum cables - connect every room but avoid loops.
